raw_consumer_statement:
In XXXX, I experienced a major issue with a XXXX XXXX  that was cancelled multiple times and delayed for over 24 hours. As a result, I was forced to sleep in the airport with no support or reasonable accommodations provided. I attempted to dispute this charge through American Express, but their process has been frustrating and opaque.

Amexs procedures have made it extremely difficult to communicate or stay informed about the status of the dispute. They do not send updates via email or mail, which forces me to manually check the portal without knowing if any new developments have occurredmaking it easy to miss key deadlines. On one occasion, Amex left a voicemail asking me to call back to discuss the dispute. I promptly called back twice, but never received a return call or follow-up. Eventually, Amex updated their portal without any notice, stating that my dispute had been declined. 

Their rationale was that I had been rebooked, but this rebooking was forced upon me, did not meet the original service I paid for, and resulted in severe inconvenience, including an overnight stay in the airport. According to Deltas own policies, passengers are entitled to a full refund if a mechanical issue causes a delay of more than 4 hourswhich clearly applies here.

I am requesting that Amex reevaluate this dispute and provide the refund I am owed for the affected portion of my travel. I would also appreciate a direct phone call to discuss the matter, as was originally promised. As a long-time cardholder, I had hoped to rely on Amexs customer service and protections, but this experience has been deeply disappointing. 

Lastly, I would like to raise a potential conflict of interest : this Amex card is co-branded with XXXX, the airline involved in the dispute. This may have influenced how my case was handled and should be reviewed by your office. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.
